stimulus: Metallurgist Pilar Echeverría and her colleagues hoped they could enhance the high-entropy alloy CoFeMnNi, an equal-proportions mixture of cobalt (Co), iron (Fe), manganese (Mn), and nickel (Ni), by substituting niobium for manganese ______blank the alloy that emerged, CoFeNbNi, proved far too brittle to perform as a replacement for CoFeMnNi.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The two clauses ("Metallurgist...(Nb)" and "the alloy that emerged...CoFeMnNi") are independent. A comma plus the coordinating conjunction "but" correctly joins them.

Choice B is incorrect because a comma alone produces a comma splice. Choice C is incorrect because the coordinating conjunction must be preceded by a comma when joining two longer main clauses. Choice D is incorrect because the unpunctuated junction produces a run-on.
